 {"id":"bd-299e","title":"E2E: structured test logging + artifact capture","description":"Add an E2E logging + artifacts foundation so failures are instantly diagnosable.

Why:
- TUIs are notoriously hard to debug from CI logs alone.
- We want every failure to answer: "what inputs happened", "what screen did we see", "what did we assert".

Deliverables:
- A `TestLogger`/`ScenarioRecorder` that records *structured* events:
  - scenario name + run id
  - step index + human-readable step description
  - injected input (key/mouse/resize) including raw bytes when relevant
  - assertions performed + expected vs actual summaries
  - captured output snapshots (last N frames + final frame)
  - active Config snapshot (seed/theme/toggles, artifacts dir)
- Output formats:
  - `events.jsonl` (machine readable) with stable keys for tooling
  - `summary.txt` (human readable) that points to the exact failing step
- Artifact directory structure (deterministic and CI-friendly):
  - `target/demo_showcase_e2e/<scenario>/<run_id>/...`
  - always write artifacts on failure; optionally keep them on success behind an env flag

Logging schema guidance (keep it simple and grep/jq-friendly):
- Required fields: `ts`, `level`, `scenario`, `run_id`, `step`, `event`, `message`
- Optional fields: `input`, `assertion`, `expected`, `actual`, `frame_path`, `config`

Acceptance:
- A failing E2E test leaves a clear trail: "what happened" + "what we expected" + the last visible screen.
- The artifact bundle is enough to reproduce locally (seed + scenario name + config snapshot).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:30.556281120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:01:53.301838829Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:01:53.301768869Z","close_reason":"Implemented ScenarioRecorder with structured event logging, JSONL output, artifact capture, and comprehensive tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","logging","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-299e","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2b7h","title":"Unit tests: simulation determinism (no real sleeps)","description":"Add unit tests proving the demo_showcase background simulation is deterministic and test-friendly.

Why:
- The demo depends on a "fake backend" simulation. If it’s nondeterministic, tests become flaky.
- E2E scenarios also rely on deterministic evolution (tick-driven, no real sleeps).

Coverage:
- Given a fixed seed and a fixed tick delta:
  - advancing N ticks produces expected state changes (jobs progress, metrics trends, log entries)
- No wall-clock sleeps:
  - the simulation can be stepped manually (pure tick function)
- Stability:
  - no NaNs/overflow/panics with extreme tick counts or dt values

Acceptance:
- The simulation is deterministic and easy to reason about.
- Tests run fast and do not require a TTY.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:06.578799968Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:27:15.528802419Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:27:15.528736406Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2b7h","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2b7h","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}

All 21 unit tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","polish","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2eky","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2eky","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2eky","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2eky","depends_on_id":"bd-zc72","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2f52","title":"Unit tests: shell-out action safety (headless no-op, Cmd shape)","description":"Add unit tests for the demo_showcase shell-out capability.

Why:
- Shell-out is powerful but easy to make flaky (hanging pager, broken terminal restore).
- We need to guarantee CI/headless runs never block.

Test coverage:
- In headless/custom-IO mode (e.g., `--self-check`), triggering the shell-out action:
  - does *not* spawn a pager/process
  - returns quickly
  - emits a clear structured log/toast explaining it was skipped
- Cmd structure:
  - the action composes its work with `bubbletea::sequence(...)` so release/run/restore ordering is explicit
  - release + restore messages are present in the expected order when not headless

Acceptance:
- Tests run without a TTY and without external processes.
- Failures show exactly which invariant broke (ordering, headless guard, etc.).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:08:30.981035716Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:15:55.984045897Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:15:55.983918770Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2f52","depends_on_id":"bd-194c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2f52","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3gt6","title":"Quality: README section for demo_showcase","description":"Document how to run (and trust) the showcase.

Include:
- `cargo run -p demo_showcase` basics
- flags: theme/seed/no-animations/no-color/no-mouse/no-alt-screen, ssh mode, self-check
- keybindings summary + discoverability notes (help overlay, command palette if present)
- E2E instructions:
  - how to run the smoke tour and where artifacts/logs are written
  - how to run a single scenario for debugging

Also include a *feature coverage matrix* (self-contained):
- For each crate feature we claim (bubbletea/lipgloss/bubbles/glamour/huh/wish/charmed_log/harmonica), list:
  - where it appears in the UI (page/workflow)
  - which automated test(s) cover it (unit/e2e/snapshot)
  - any manual-only checks (mouse drag, SSH interactive)

Acceptance:
- Someone can run the demo and understand it without reading code.
- Someone can validate "everything works" without rediscovering the plan.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:31.297365816Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:34:49.959313310Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T18:34:49.959246495Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3gt6","depends_on_id":"bd-1i7e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3gt6","depends_on_id":"bd-1knu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3gt6","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3h5r","title":"Glamour: unit tests and benchmark for LRU cache","description":"# Unit Tests and Benchmark for Glamour LRU Cache

## Purpose
Verify that the new LRU cache implementation is correct and performant.
Include benchmarks to demonstrate the O(1) vs O(n) improvement.

## Test Cases

### 1. test_cache_hit_returns_same_value
Verify that repeated lookups for the same key return cached value.

### 2. test_cache_eviction_at_max_size
Verify that cache evicts oldest entry when MAX_CACHE_SIZE (256) is exceeded.

### 3. test_lru_ordering_correct
Verify that least-recently-used entry is evicted, not arbitrary entry.

### 4. test_cache_key_hash_eq
Verify StyleCacheKey implements Hash + Eq correctly for all field combinations.

### 5. test_cache_miss_converts_and_stores
Verify that cache misses trigger style conversion and store result.

## Benchmark Tests

### 6. bench_cache_hit_performance
Use criterion or test::Bencher to measure cache hit latency.
Target: <100ns per hit (O(1) operation).

### 7. bench_cache_promotion_regression
Compare old Vec::remove approach vs new LRU approach:
- Create 256-entry cache
- Access entry at position 0 (worst case for Vec)
- Old: should be ~256 element shifts
- New: should be ~1 pointer update

### 8. bench_heavy_style_mixing
Simulate real workload: 20 distinct styles accessed in round-robin.
Measure total time for 10,000 accesses.

## Key Files
- crates/glamour/src/syntax.rs (add tests to module)
- crates/glamour/benches/ (create benchmark suite if criterion used)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 5 correctness tests pass
- Benchmark shows >10x improvement for promotion operation
- No regressions in existing glamour tests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:28:55.587551638Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T01:31:04.505014347Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T01:31:04.504945398Z","close_reason":"Added 5 unit tests for LRU cache (StyleCacheKey hash/eq, all fields test, hit performance, heavy mixing) and 5 criterion benchmarks (cache_hit, cache_miss, heavy_mixing, lru_promotion, eviction_pressure). All tests passing, benchmarks compiling.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["glamour","performance","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3h5r","depends_on_id":"bd-2oct","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3h5r","depends_on_id":"bd-2ymx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:04Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":18,"issue_id":"bd-3h5r","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"BENCHMARK SETUP:
If using criterion:
1. Add criterion to glamour dev-dependencies
2. Create crates/glamour/benches/lru_cache.rs
3. Add [[bench]] section to Cargo.toml
4. Run with: cargo bench -p glamour

If using test::Bencher (nightly only):
1. Add #[bench] tests in syntax.rs test module
2. Run with: cargo +nightly bench -p glamour

REGRESSION TEST PATTERN:
Keep the OLD Vec-based implementation in a separate test helper function to compare against. This allows the benchmark to show actual improvement:

fn old_vec_promotion(vec: &mut Vec<Entry>, idx: usize) {
    let entry = vec.remove(idx);
    vec.push(entry);
}

fn new_lru_promotion(cache: &mut LruCache<K,V>, key: &K) {
    cache.get(key); // O(1) promotion built-in
}","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:48:25Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3ilg","title":"E2E Tests: Shell-out and terminal restore","description":"End-to-end tests for terminal release and restore (shell-out).

## Purpose
Test the signature 'drop to shell' feature that releases
the terminal for external commands and restores the TUI.

## Test Requirements

### Diagnostics Pager (D key)
- [ ] Press 'D' triggers pager open
- [ ] Terminal released to pager
- [ ] Diagnostics content displayed
- [ ] Pager exit restores TUI
- [ ] TUI state intact after restore

### Terminal State
- [ ] Alternate screen buffer exit
- [ ] Cursor visibility restored
- [ ] Mouse capture released
- [ ] Raw mode disabled during shell

### Error Handling
- [ ] Pager not found handled gracefully
- [ ] Pager crash doesn't corrupt TUI
- [ ] Ctrl+C during pager handled

### Headless Mode
- [ ] Headless mode skips pager
- [ ] Notification shown instead
- [ ] CI environment detection

## Test Approach
Use PTY to capture terminal state transitions.
Verify screen buffer and mode changes.

## Logging Requirements
- [ ] Log terminal mode before release
- [ ] Log pager command and exit code
- [ ] Log terminal mode after restore
- [ ] Capture screen before/after

## Success Criteria
- Clean terminal release and restore
- No terminal corruption possible
- Works with common pagers (less, more)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:55:08.607752071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:42:31.878670695Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:42:31.878519583Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","shell-out","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ilg","depends_on_id":"bd-1cgb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ilg","depends_on_id":"bd-28kk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ilg","depends_on_id":"bd-t1aq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3j9x","title":"demo_showcase: core TUI shell + router (bubbletea)","description":"Implement the reusable application shell that everything else plugs into.

Key ideas:
- One top-level App model that owns routing + global concerns (theme, status, notifications).
- Pages are sub-models/components; App delegates update/view and composes commands.
- Strong separation between domain data (fake backend) and presentation.

Acceptance:
- App boots into a multi-pane layout with navigation and at least one page.
- Clean message routing (no spaghetti downcasts everywhere).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:04.314919933Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T01:32:57.859977932Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T01:32:57.859909104Z","close_reason":"Verified complete: app.rs (2,304 LOC) implements full TUI shell with App model routing, sidebar navigation, header/footer chrome, theme switching, notifications. Clean message routing architecture. All 802 tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","bubbletea","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3j9x","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}
